project.name: Anna's Farm — Common Space Entry Landscape
project.deliverable_summary: Professional client proposal (PDF + HTML one-pager) with line-item estimate, GIS site context, site photos, AI mockup renders, parametric Houdini 3D scene, optional Veo 3 walkthrough video, optional Claw3D browser presentation.

client.name: TBD
client.notes: Client details pending — will fill in before final proposal delivery.

location.city_region: Lafayette, Boulder County, Colorado — Anna's Farm subdivision, west of Hwy 287 on Baseline Rd
location.address: Anna's Farm subdivision, Lafayette, CO — two common-space entry sites
location.cross_streets: Baseline Rd / Anna Thomas Pkwy / Homestead St
gis_sources_planned: Boulder County GIS Open Data — parcel + aerial
OpenStreetMap Overpass — street network, nearby features
Google Street View or Mapillary — ground-level reference

sites.name: Site One — Common Space Entry A
sites.area_note: GIS-confirmed polygon = 3,500 sqft, matches quote exactly.

line_items.item: River rock & granite stones (dry creek bed)
Colorado red flagstone — vertical Flatirons-style installation

sites.name: Site Two — Common Space Entry B
sites.area_note: GIS polygon measures 1,567 sqft; billing at 1,500 sqft per owner confirmation.

line_items.item: River rock & granite stones (dry creek bed)
Colorado red flagstone — vertical Flatirons-style installation

style_direction.aesthetic: Colorado Front Range site-specific geological sculpture, Flatirons-inspired vertical stone installation, low-water xeriscape, HOA common-entry landmark

design_concept.summary: A site-specific geological sculpture referencing the Flatirons of Boulder, using native Colorado materials as a landmark for the community entry. Two distinct flagstone elements: the vertical Flatirons formation (hero) and smaller standing stepping stones in a semi-arc.
design_concept.hero_element: Vertical Colorado red flagstone slabs installed upright, varied height and tilt, clustered to create a mountain ridge silhouette — the Flatirons formation

stepping_stones.description: Colorado red flagstone pieces, 6-8 inches wide, standing 1-2 feet above grade, lined up side by side in a semi-arc pattern, randomly placed throughout the planting bed
stepping_stones.note: These are NOT flat walkable pavers — they stand upright like smaller echoes of the main Flatirons formation

visual_hierarchy: 1. Vertical Flatirons formation (hero / dominant sculptural element)
2. Standing stepping stones in semi-arc (secondary vertical rhythm)
4. Dry creek flow (river rock simulating erosion around formation)

spatial_composition.centerpiece_zone: Flatirons flagstone formation — vertical red slabs in clustered ridge
spatial_composition.stepping_stone_arc: Semi-arc of 6-8 inch wide stones standing 1-2 ft above grade, randomly placed through planting bed
spatial_composition.flow_field: Dry creek bed wrapping through and around the slabs
spatial_composition.edge_conditions: Clean transitions to sidewalk and existing turf
spatial_composition.negative_space: Open gravel areas to emphasize verticality of stone

palette.ground_riverbed: river rock / granite stones shaped as a flowing dry creek bed — the primary ground feature
palette.ground_surround: contrasting decorative stone, 2"–6" diameter, on the outsides of the river bed — fills all ground area outside the creek
palette.boulders: native Colorado granite and river boulders — anchoring and scale contrast
palette.flagstone_slabs: Colorado red flagstone — LARGE vertical slabs composed like Flatirons mountains, per concept drawing
palette.flagstone_arc: Colorado red flagstone pieces — smaller, 6-8" wide, 1-2 ft above grade, lined up side by side in semi-arc, also vertical, mountain range silhouette on top edge
palette.strip_stone: Colorado red strip stone — base transitions around vertical elements
palette.key_rule: ALL flagstone and slabs are VERTICAL. Top edges have mountain range / Flatirons profile. The ONLY things on the ground are the river bed stones and the contrasting decorative stone surrounding them.

style_direction.vegetation: Scope is hardscape only — no sod, no planting, no irrigation. Mockups may include existing adjacent vegetation for context.

mockup_shots.label: Completed install — wide daytime (matches before framing)
Dusk / blue hour — solar lights glowing

walkthrough_video.style: slow cinematic push-in from entry approach to full reveal, native ambient audio

houdini_3d.representation: instanced vertical geometry, NOT surface polygons

vertical_flagstone.type: VerticalStone instances — position, height, width, rotation, tilt
vertical_flagstone.clustering: Flatirons-style ridge silhouette, varied height and tilt
vertical_flagstone.generation: procedural Flatirons clustering algorithm with height + tilt variation

houdini_3d.boulder_placement: native granite anchors for scale contrast, hand + PDG Wedge variants
houdini_3d.dry_creek: river rock flow field wrapping through and around vertical slab formation

pascal_editor.use: If the Pascal editor integration is confirmed, author one parametric template and expose brief.json fields as editor parameters.

reasoning_engine_notes.use: Route the brief through the Pixeltable RAG + doc_query loop to pull any relevant prior projects, material pricing references, and HOA design guidance before authoring prompts.
reasoning_engine_notes.critique_loop: After mockups land on disk, run the visual critique pipeline (Gemma4-Visual-Agent) and feed the JSON back into the next prompt iteration.
